dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 6732 : Coercion and Sexual Abuse Free Environment Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Received; read twice and referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To prohibit unlawful coercion of a minor, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Coercion and Sexual Abuse Free Environment Act or the CSAFE Act.

subsection.text: Section 2422 of title 18, United States Code, is amended by adding at the end the following:

paragraph.text: Whoever, using the mail or any facility or means of interstate or foreign commerce, or within the special maritime and territorial jurisdiction of the United States, intentionally compels a minor to—

subparagraph.text: engage in self-harm, including suicide or attempted suicide;
commit animal crushing (as defined in section 48);
engage in abusive or degrading nonsexual conduct (whether or not physical injury results) for which any person can be charged with a criminal offense; or
engage in sexually explicit conduct, as defined in section 2256(2)(A),

subsection.continuation-text: shall be punished as provided in paragraph (2).

paragraph.text: Whoever violates, attempts to violate, or conspires to violate paragraph (1)—

subparagraph.text: shall be fined under this title, imprisoned not more than 10 years, or both;
if serious bodily injury results, shall be fined under this title, imprisoned not more than 20 years, or both; or
if death results, shall be fined under this title, imprisoned for any term of years or for life, or both.

paragraph.text: In this subsection, the term compel includes the use of a threat, extortion, blackmail, fraud, deceit, or manipulation.

subsection.text: Section 5032 of title 18, United States Code, is amended by striking or 2241(c), and inserting 2241(c), or 2242(c),.

attestation-group.attestation-date: Passed the House of Representatives January 12, 2026.
